summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's graphics configuration improve support for video playback and streaming. The addition of a **PDMFactorySupport singleton** enhances the way the browser manages video codecs and ensures that settings are consistently applied across sessions. This is particularly beneficial for the **Media Capabilities API**, which checks the capabilities of users' devices for smoother playback and better video quality. Additionally, Firefox now allows High Dynamic Range (HDR) video playback on Windows for users with AMD and NVIDIA graphics cards, enabling a richer visual experience for supported videos. These changes are important as they help Firefox keep up with modern multimedia standards, improving user experience during video calls and streaming.
